name: Hamas
description: Palestinian Sunni Islamist militant organization and political party governing the Gaza Strip since 2007. Its military wing, the Izz ad-Din al-Qassam Brigades, carried out the October 7, 2023 attack on southern Israel — the deadliest single attack in Israeli history. Hamas receives significant financial and military support from Iran.

name: Hezbollah
territory: Southern Lebanon, Beqaa Valley, southern suburbs of Beirut
description: Lebanese Shia Islamist political party and militant group. The most heavily armed non-state actor in the world. Functions as a state-within-a-state in Lebanon with its own military, social services, and political representation. Primary proxy of Iran's 'Axis of Resistance.' Engaged in cross-border attacks on Israel since October 8, 2023.

name: Houthis / Ansar Allah
territory: Northwestern Yemen including Sanaa, Hodeidah, Saada
description: Yemeni armed movement controlling northwestern Yemen. Since November 2023, has conducted over 100 attacks on commercial and military shipping in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden in claimed solidarity with Palestinians. Attacks have disrupted global shipping, forcing major carriers to reroute around Africa. US and UK have conducted extensive strikes on Houthi positions.

name: Palestinian Islamic Jihad
description: Palestinian militant organization closely aligned with Iran. Second largest armed group in Gaza after Hamas. Participated in the October 7 attack. Focuses exclusively on armed resistance without the political governance role that Hamas maintains.

name: ISIS / Islamic State
fullName: ad-Dawlah al-Islamiyah fi al-Iraq wa ash-Sham
aliases: Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant
territory: No significant territory held (formerly controlled Mosul, Raqqa, large swaths of Iraq/Syria)
description: Transnational Salafi-jihadist militant group that at its peak in 2014-2015 controlled territory across Iraq and Syria, declaring a 'caliphate.' Largely defeated militarily by 2019 through US-led coalition and local forces. Continues insurgent operations and maintains global affiliate networks across Africa, Central Asia, and Southeast Asia.

name: IRGC
fullName: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (Sepah-e Pasdaran-e Enqelab-e Eslami)
territory: Iran (national military); operational presence across Middle East
description: Branch of the Iranian Armed Forces founded after the 1979 Islamic Revolution. The IRGC's Quds Force is responsible for extraterritorial operations and managing Iran's network of proxy militias across the Middle East — including Hezbollah, Hamas, Houthis, and Iraqi Shia militias. Iran's primary instrument of power projection and the architect of the 'Axis of Resistance.'

name: Al-Qaeda
fullName: Tanzim Qaidat al-Jihad fi Kull al-Alam
territory: No controlled territory; operational presence through affiliates worldwide
annualBudget: $20-50 million (estimated, through donations and affiliate revenue)
description: Founded by Osama bin Laden. Responsible for the September 11, 2001 attacks. Core leadership severely degraded by US counterterrorism operations. Now primarily a decentralized network operating through regional affiliates: AQAP (Yemen), AQIM (North Africa), JNIM (Sahel), Al-Shabaab (East Africa). Leader Ayman al-Zawahiri killed by US drone strike in Kabul, July 2022.

name: Al-Shabaab
description: Al-Qaeda's affiliate in East Africa. Controls significant territory in south-central Somalia and generates substantial revenue through taxation and extortion. Conducts frequent attacks in Somalia and neighboring Kenya. US AFRICOM conducts regular drone strikes against Al-Shabaab leadership and operatives.

name: Kata'ib Hezbollah
fullName: Kata'ib Hezbollah (Brigades of the Party of God)
description: Iranian-backed Iraqi Shia militia and one of the most powerful groups within Iraq's Popular Mobilization Forces (PMF). Has conducted numerous rocket and drone attacks on US forces in Iraq and Syria. Responsible for the January 2024 drone attack that killed 3 US soldiers at Tower 22 in Jordan.

name: Wagner Group / Africa Corps
fullName: PMC Wagner (now restructured as Africa Corps under GRU)
stateSponsors: Russia (GRU/MOD — restructured under military control after 2023 mutiny)
territory: Operational presence in Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Libya, CAR, Mozambique, Syria
description: Russian private military company founded by Yevgeny Prigozhin (killed August 2023). After the failed June 2023 mutiny, Wagner was restructured under Russian military intelligence (GRU) as 'Africa Corps.' Operates across sub-Saharan Africa providing military support to ruling juntas in exchange for mining concessions. Committed widespread human rights abuses in Mali, CAR, and Libya.

name: PKK
territory: Qandil Mountains (Iraq-Iran border); affiliated forces in NE Syria (SDF/YPG)
description: Kurdish militant organization fighting for Kurdish autonomy/independence, primarily against Turkey. Has been engaged in armed conflict with the Turkish state since 1984. Turkey conducts extensive cross-border operations against PKK positions in northern Iraq and targets PKK-affiliated groups in Syria.

name: Boko Haram
aliases: ISWAP (Islamic State West Africa Province — splinter)
People Committed to the Prophet's Teachings
description: Nigerian jihadist insurgent group responsible for the 2014 Chibok schoolgirls kidnapping. Has killed over 35,000 people and displaced 2 million. ISWAP, a splinter faction pledging allegiance to ISIS, has become the dominant faction after the death of Boko Haram leader Abubakar Shekau in 2021.

name: Taliban
stateSponsors: Pakistan (ISI — historical and ongoing alleged support)
annualBudget: Government budget: $2+ billion (mining, taxation, drug trade)

usDesignation.status: Not designated as FTO (Haqqani Network separately designated)

description: Islamist political and military movement that ruled Afghanistan 1996-2001, was overthrown by US invasion, fought a 20-year insurgency, and retook control of Afghanistan in August 2021 following US withdrawal. Now the de facto government. Continues to face armed resistance from ISIS-K (Islamic State Khorasan Province) and NRF (National Resistance Front).

name: TTP
description: Pakistani Taliban umbrella organization conducting insurgency against the Pakistani state. Responsible for the 2014 Peshawar school massacre (132 children killed). Has intensified operations since the Afghan Taliban takeover, using Afghanistan as a safe haven despite Taliban denials.

name: Popular Mobilization Forces
territory: Iraq — particularly Nineveh, Saladin, Diyala provinces

usDesignation.status: Key constituent groups designated (Kata'ib Hezbollah, AAH)

description: Iraqi state-sanctioned umbrella organization of mostly Shia militias formed in 2014 to fight ISIS. Key Iranian-aligned components include Kata'ib Hezbollah, Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq, Badr Organization, and Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba. Several constituent groups have attacked US forces in Iraq and Syria with rockets and drones since October 2023.

name: JNIM
fullName: Jama'at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (Group for Supporting Islam and Muslims)
territory: Northern and central Mali, northern Burkina Faso, western Niger
description: Al-Qaeda's affiliate in the Sahel, formed by the merger of Ansar Dine, the Macina Liberation Front, Al-Mourabitoun, and AQIM's Saharan branch. The dominant jihadist force in the Sahel region, engaged in insurgency across Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger. Has expanded operations even as Wagner/Africa Corps deploys to the region.
